species.citation: (Wight) Alston , Handb. Fl. Ceylon 6(Suppl.) (1931) 188; ≡ Symplocos pendula Wight , Icon. Pl. Ind. Orient. (Wight) 4 (1848) t.1237 ; Nooteboom, Fl. (Engler) IV, 242 (1901) 88 ; Brand, PJS 3c (1908) 3; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 297. = Symplocos foxworthyi Brand , PJS 3c (1908) 3 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 299. = Bobua confusa (Brand) Kaneh. & Sasaki , List Pl. Formosa (Sasaki) (1928) 330.
species.distribution: China, India, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Vietnam. DINAGAT (photos), LUZON: Mountain Province (photos), Nueva Vizcaya (photos), Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao Oriental, Misamis Oriental (photos), PALAWAN, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Primary and secondary montane and high montane mossy forests, often common on ridges. 600-3300m.

species.notes: (15-)600-2000m. var. henschelii. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, ?Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. ?PALAWAN. Notes: P.B. Pelser (27-Apr-24): Ridsdale SMHI 1695 (L) possibly represents this species in the Philippines. (130-)600-2000m.

species.notes: Throughout Malesia except Java (but found in Bawean Isl). 0-3000m.

species.citation: (Lour.) S.Moore , J. Bot. 52 (1914) 148 ; Nooteboom, Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 141; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 245; ≡ Dicalyx cochinchinensis Lour. , Fl. Cochinch. (1790) 663. var. cochinchinensis. Distribution: China, India, Indochina, Japan, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sulawesi, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Ifugao. Notes: Throughout Malesia except the Lesser Sunda Isls; scarce in the Philippines. 0-2500(-3000)m. Native. var. philippinensis (Brand) Noot., Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 154; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 247; ≡ Symplocos javanica var. philippinensis Brand, PJS 3c (1908) 6. = Symplocos ahernii Brand, PJS 3c (1908) 6; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 297. = Symplocos purpurascens Brand, PJS 7c (1912) 33; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 302. = Symplocos ramosii Merr., PJS 12c (1917) 293; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 302. Distribution: Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Camarines Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Pampanga, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, NEGROS, PALAWAN, SAMAR, SIBUYAN. Notes: Primary dipterocarp forests. 0-2000(-2400)m. Native. var. imbricata (Brand) Noot., Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 155; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 248. = Symplocos imbricata Brand, PJS 4c (1909) 109; Brand, PJS 7c (1912) 31; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 299. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya. Notes: High montane, single storey mossy forests. Fl. Dec-Apr; fr. Aug-Sep, Jan-Mar. 2000-2600m. Native.

species.notes: In a variety of habitats, also in dense mossy forests. Fl. Dec-Apr; fr. May-Dec. 400-2500m.

(1826-1827) 1116. = Symplocos acuminatissima Merr. , PJS 11c (1916) 31 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 296. = Symplocos apoensis Elmer , LPB 7 (1914) 2319 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 297. = Symplocos bulusanensis Elmer , LPB 10 (1939) 3792, nom. inval., no Latin descr. = Symplocos dagamiensis Brand , Repert. Haenk. 2 (1835) 61 ; Brand, Pflanzenreich 6 (1901) 34; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 301. = Symplocos patens C.Presl forma ciliata (C.Presl) Brand , PJS 3c (1908) 4; ≡ Symplocos ciliata C.Presl , Reliq. Haenk. 2 (1835) 61 ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 127. = Symplocos patens C.Presl forma elmeri (Brand) Brand , PJS 3c (1908) 4; ≡ Symplocos elmeri Brand , Fragm. Fl. (1886) 179; Type: Vidal 448 (MA), Luzon: San Mateo. = Symplocos pulgarensis Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1841 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 302. = Symplocos salix Brand , Repert. Spec. Nov. Regni Veg. 14 (1916) 325 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 302. = Symplocos villarii S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 178 ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 302; Type: Vidal 446 (MA, syn), Luzon: Bulacan, Angat; Vidal 828 (MA, syn), Luzon: Camarines Sur, Sipocot; Vidal 1581 (MA, syn), Luzon: Ilocos Norte. = Symplocos verdifolia Elmer , LPB 10 (1939) 3793, nom. inval., no Latin descr. ≠ Symplocos spicata auct. non Roxb. ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 127; S.Vidal, Sinopsis Atlas (1883) t. 64. var. odoratissima. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. BILIRAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Bataan, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Mountain Province, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SAMAR. Notes: Common in primary and secondary rainforests at low and medium elevation. 0-2500m. Native. var. wenzelii (Merr.) Noot., Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 248; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 267; ≡ Symplocos wenzelii Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 282; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 302. Distribution: Borneo, Philippines. LEYTE. Notes: Fl. (Mar-)Jun-Dec; fr. (Feb-May-)Jul. Native.
species.distribution: BILIRAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Aurora, Bataan, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Isabela (photos), Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SAMAR.

species.citation: C.B.Clarke , Fl. Brit. India 3 (1882) 579 ; Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 267. ssp. cumingiana (Brand) Noot., Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 253; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 268; ≡ Symplocos cumingiana Brand, Pflanzenr. (Engler) IV, 242 (1901) 58; Brand, PJS 3c (1908) 8; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 297. Distribution: BILIRAN, DINAGAT, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora (photos), Batangas, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental, Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN. Notes: (300-)500-3000(-3700)m. Native. var. cumingiana. = Symplocos angularis Elmer, LPB 2 (1908) 510. = Symplocos curtiflora Elmer, LPB 2 (1908) 509; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 298. = Symplocos elliptifolia Merr., PJS 12c (1917) 292; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 298. = Symplocos ilocana Merr., PJS 35 (1928) 7. = Symplocos minutiflora Elmer, LPB 7 (1914) 2320; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 300. = Symplocos purpurascens Brand, PJS 7c (1912) 33; EPFP 3 (1923) 302. = Symplocos agusanensis Elmer, LPB 7 (1914) 2321. ≠ Symplocos brachybotrys auct. non Merr.; Merr., PJS 14 (1919) 447; EPFP 3 (1923) 300. Distribution: Borneo, Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Aurora (photos), SIBUYAN. Notes: (300-)1000-3000(-3700)m. Native. var. pachyphylla (Merr.) Noot., Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 254; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 269; ≡ Symplocos pachyphylla Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 283; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 300. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LEYTE, MINDANAO, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN. Notes: Hill forests. Fl. Sep. 500m. Native. ssp. perakensis (King & Gamble) Noot., Leiden Bot. Ser. 1 (1975) 255; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 268; ≡ Symplocos perakensis King & Gamble, J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 74 (1906) 241; Type: from Peninsular Malaysia. Distribution: NEGROS. Notes: 60-1500m. Native. var. perakensis. = Symplocos fragrans Elmer, LPB 2 (1908) 508; Brand, PJS 7c (1912) 33; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 299. Distribution: Malay Peninsula, Philippines. NEGROS. Notes: 60-1500m. Native.
